title: Release-tuple guard + templates/lib mirror test, wired into verify
action: Create TWO standing guards and add both to .bee/config.json commands.verify (DIST-05, PROJ-08). (1) scripts/test_release_tuple.mjs: read BEE_VERSION from BOTH skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/state.mjs and .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs, plus the version from .claude-plugin/plugin.json and .codex-plugin/plugin.json; assert all four equal; exit 1 naming any desynced component. Include an internal self-test proving the checker flags a synthetic desynced tuple (no mutation of real files). (2) scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs: derive the file set via readdirSync of skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/, assert each file byte-identical to its .bee/bin/lib/ counterpart AND that neither side has extra/missing files; exit 1 with the diffing filenames on any mismatch, exit 0 when identical. Include a self-test proving an injected byte-diff is detected (against a temp copy, not the real tree). Append both to commands.verify.
verify: node scripts/test_release_tuple.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node -e "const v=require('./.bee/config.json').commands.verify||''; if(!v.includes('test_release_tuple.mjs')||!v.includes('test_lib_mirror.mjs')) throw new Error('scripts not wired into commands.verify field'); console.log('WIRED-OK');" && echo TUPLE-MIRROR-PASS

truths: test_release_tuple.mjs asserts BEE_VERSION (templates/lib + .bee/bin/lib) == both plugin.json versions; exit 1 naming any desync; passes now (all 0.1.44)
test_release_tuple.mjs self-test proves it flags a synthetic desynced tuple (no real-file mutation)
test_lib_mirror.mjs derives file set via readdirSync (no hand-list), asserts templates/lib <-> .bee/bin/lib byte-identity + no extra/missing files; exit 1 with filenames on mismatch; passes now
test_lib_mirror.mjs self-test proves an injected byte-diff is detected (temp copy, not real tree)
both scripts appended to .bee/config.json commands.verify; full verify chain still exits 0
prohibitions: do not hand-enumerate the lib file set

PASS test_lib_mirror: internal self-test — checker correctly flags injected byte-diff, missing file, and extra file
